title: Slipper
content:
When I was very young
        onto school,
        a slick of water curled
        under a behemoth, silver poplar tree ...
        there, white underbacks
        of leaves waved in showy pride the
        dead underbellies of bass ...
        as tall boys,
        big with rakish, probing, anthracite eyes,
        stooped in the creek
        their red, exposed flesh
        colour of school brick.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "Slipper", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
